text.title: RAG Evaluation — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the rag-evaluation methodology. Sources: Es et al. — RAGAS framework (arXiv:2309.15217); Shahul et al. — RAG evaluation taxonomy; Pinecone — RAG evaluation best practices.

rule.statement: Require stable document IDs before computing retrieval metrics; hash(content) is acceptable, raw chunk text is not.
rule.rationale: Precision@K and Recall@K require matching retrieved doc IDs against relevance labels; without IDs the calculation is impossible.

rule.statement: Separate retrieval evaluation from generation evaluation in every run; a combined score masks root cause and misdirects fixes.

rationale.source: Shahul et al. — RAG evaluation taxonomy

rule.rationale: Retrieval and generation have independent failure modes; conflating them produces 'high faithfulness, low recall' diagnoses fixed wrongly.

rule.statement: Pass these baselines before production: Faithfulness > 0.85, Answer Relevance > 0.90, Hit Rate > 0.90.
rule.rationale: Thresholds represent consistently observed quality across diverse production RAG systems; ship below them and customers will notice.

rule.statement: If using synthetic queries, generate them with a different model than the one being evaluated.
rule.rationale: Same-model synthesis creates self-fulfilling evaluations; the evaluated model scores artificially high on its own generated questions.

rule.statement: Report confidence intervals (or repeated-run variance) for all LLM-as-judge metrics; a single-run number is misleading.
rule.rationale: LLM-as-judge varies ±3–5% between runs; single-point estimates over-state quality and under-state risk.
